rulings.Case Title: ROBERT HALF INTERNATIONAL, INC. A DELAWARE CORPOR VS. DONNA FARRUGIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 1, 2015, Line 10, CROSS DEFENDANT ROBERT HALF INTERNATIONAL, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT. Motion to Strike is denied. Under CCP §436, movant must show the pleading to contain irrelevant, false, or improper matter, the Court finds the Cross-Complaint does not contain irrelevant, false, or improper matter. CCP § 475 states: the court must, in every stage of an action, disregard any error, improper ruling, instruction, or defect, in the pleadings or proceedings which, in the opinion of said court, does not affect the substantial rights of the parties. Here, the Court finds Defendant's untimely filing of the cross complaint does not prejudice Plaintiff, further the Court finds Defendant's error resulting in the late filing to be excusable. Thus, Plaintiff's motion to strike is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL B. HILLEGEIST VS. F/V SUSAN T. INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 1, 2015, Line 11, DEFENDANT PATRICK DAVIS, F/V SUSAN T. INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. The motion for summary adjudication is denied. Plaintiff characterizes the injury as purely latent. The time of event rule applies to patent injuries or where the plaintiff has sustained both immediate and latent injuries. The discovery rule applies to purely latent injuries. "A pure latent injury case is one in which 'the plaintiff fails to discover either the injury or its cause until long after the negligent act occurred.'" Clay v. Union Carbide Corp., 828 F.2d 1103, 1106 (5th Cir. 1987). At par. 7 of plaintiff's declaration, he explains that he did not experience any discomfort until a week after the incident. If the statute of limitations commenced at that time, then the claims are timely. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether plaintiff did or should have discerned injury on June 29, 2011, and whether the discovery rule applies. Therefore, there is a triable issue of material fact whether the 3-year statute of limitations bars the Jones Act and unseaworthiness claims. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: DANEAL HARRIS VS. RONNIE BELL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order To Compel Nueropsycholigical Exam Without Recording Of Testing
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 1, 2015, Line 12, DEFENDANT RONNIE BELL, MONICA BELL'S Motion For Order To Compel Nueropsycholigical Exam Without Recording Of Testing. Defendants' motion is granted in part and denied in part as follows: (1) The motion is granted to authorize an independent neuropsychological examination pursuant to C.C.P. Section 2032.310. The examination is deemed to be a mental examination. Hearing required to determine the scope of the examination, the tests to be administered, and the terms of any protective order governing disclosure of the examination results or the proceedings. The parties are to meet and confer on the factors referenced in C.C.P. Section 2032.310(b), the terms of an appropriate protective order, and must specify in the order granting this motion the exact tests to be administered in the examination. The court will review the tests to be administered and may deny giving any particular test where good cause exists to do so. (2) The motion is denied to the extent that defendants seek to bar audio recording of the testing phase of the examination. The court finds that C.C.P. Section 2032.530 governs the recording of the test session. Defendants fail to carry their burden to demonstrate that federal copyright laws preempted application of the state discovery statutes. The audiotaping of the entire session may be conducted by the examiner. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: MISTI JOHNSON VS. HONG ZHOU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Be Relieved From Waiver Of Objections To Written Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 1, 2015, Line 13, PLAINTIFF MISTI JOHNSON'S Motion To Be Relieved From Waiver Of Objections To Written Discovery. Plaintiff Johnson's Motion to be Relieved from Waiver of Objections to Written Discovery is GRANTED. Unless the error is based on mistake, inadvertence, or excusable neglect, a party who fails to timely respond to requests for Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, and Request for Production of Document waives "any objection" to these requests, "including one based on privilege" or the protection of attorney work product. (Code Civ. Pro. §§ 2030.290-2031.300.) The purpose of similar mandatory relief provisions is to alleviate the hardship on parties who suffer due to an inexcusable failure to act by their attorneys. (Zamora v. Clayborn Contracting Group, Inc. (2002) 28 Cal.4th 249, 257.) It is, therefore, within this Court's discretion to grant Plaintiff Johnson's motion on the grounds that her error in timely filing discovery responses was due to the mistake, inadvertence, or excusable neglect by prior counsel. Plaintiff Johnson substituted counsel after her former counsel failed to provide timely discovery responses. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: THOMAS F MCCORMICK VS. SECURITY MUTUAL LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY OF NEW YORK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Security Mutual Life Insurance Comp Any Of New York To:
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 1, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF THOMAS MCCORMICK'S Motion To Compel Defendant Security Mutual Life Insurance Company Of New York To: (1) Comply with the Court's June 3, 2015 Order; (2) Produce Further Responses to Plaintiff's Requests for Production of Documents, Set Three; (3) Produce Its Person(s) Most Knowledgeable for Deposition in San Francisco; and (4) For Monetary Sanctions Against Security Mutual. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear to discuss why delay in producing documents after court order and status of those documents mentioned in reply on pages 2-3 and issue of custody and control as to CSC documents, as well as issue of whether sanctions should be ordered and, if so, in what amount. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com,a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
